strengths: System prompt (llm.ts:230-330) faithfully reproduces the spec's Scope Guard System Prompt draft and all four Few-Shot examples (ASK/REFINE/NEW_DIRECTION/OFF_TOPIC) verbatim, plus a sandwich JSON-only constraint at start and end for robust parsing.
Disambiguation rules enforced per spec: ASK-over-REFINE and REFINE-over-NEW_DIRECTION (llm.ts:268-269); OFF_TOPIC only for clearly unrelated requests.

Multi-strategy JSON parsing (llm.ts:402-447) mirrors the established classifyIntentOnce pattern: direct parse -> code-block -> greedy object -> field extraction, with a normalizeFollowUpResult validator that rejects invalid intents.
Retry logic correctly differs from classifyIntent as specified: retries only on parse failure (no semantic short-circuit), up to 3 attempts, then returns a fixed OFF_TOPIC fallback (llm.ts:455-489).
Role/context injection correctly limits artifact (not all column outputs) per Design Decision 2 — an intentional, spec-aligned refinement of the draft prompt.
TDD respected: tests added before implementation; all 34 tests pass (10 new classifyFollowUpIntent tests). Pure async, no DB deps. Only llm.ts and llm.test.ts changed.

issues.description: Strategy-4 field-extraction unescaping only unescapes quotes; other JSON escapes (\n, \\) would remain literal. Acceptable as a last-resort fallback (strategies 1-3 handle well-formed JSON), and not exercised by spec scenarios.
